title: rhamnus frangula subsp. baetica
intro: Rhamnus frangula subsp. baetica, also known as Baetic buckthorn, is a subspecies of the Rhamnus frangula plant, a type of shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its laxative and purgative effects, which are attributed to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Rhamnus frangula subsp. baetica include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve constipation, and support the treatment of various gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome. Rhamnus frangula subsp. baetica can be prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of tea, capsules, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to its potential interactions and side effects.
